title: Athera AI Summit Chief Guest Agenda

chunks.text: ATHERA AI SUMMIT Chief Guest Agenda Full Day Schedule Time Duration Segment 8:00 – 8:30 AM 30 min Inauguration 8:30 – 8:45 AM 15 min Chief Guest arrival (attendees assemble at venues) 8:45 – 9:00 AM 15 min Seating / settle buffer 9:00 – 9:45 AM 45 min Chief Guest addressing 9:45 AM – 1:05 PM 3h 15m AI Summit — Panel Discussion Block (5 topics × 40 min) 1:05 – 1:20 PM 15 min Prep for closing 1:20 – 2:05 PM 45 min Closing / Valedictory Note: At 40 min per topic × 5 topics, the panel block runs 9:45 AM – 1:00 PM. Closing has been shifted accordingly to keep the day intact. Panel Block Timestamps Time Topic 9:45 – 10:25 AM Topic 1 — State of AI: From Research Labs to Real-World Deployment 10:25 – 11:05 AM Topic 2 — Building AI Products for the Indian Market 11:05 – 11:45 AM Topic 3 — Applied AI in Industry: A Case Study 11:45 AM – 12:25 PM Topic 4 — From Idea to Startup: Funding, GTM, Scaling 12:25 – 1:05 PM Topic 5 — The Future of AI: Responsible Building & What Comes Next Topic Details 1. State of AI: From Research Labs to Real-World Deployment Current model landscape, what's hype vs. production-ready, where the frontier actually is. Good opening topic to set shared context for the audience. 2. Building AI Products for the Indian Market: What Works, What Doesn't Localization challenges, cost-of-inference constraints, Tier 2/3 user behavior, pricing for the Indian market — practical for students building their own products. 3. Applied AI in Industry: A Case Study from [Embedded/Vision/Enterprise]

Deep technical dive into one real deployed system (model choices, constraints, failure modes) — showing AI beyond chatbots (e.g., computer vision, edge AI). 4. From Idea to Startup: Funding, GTM, and Scaling an AI Venture in India VC/angel landscape for AI startups in India, go-to-market lessons, hiring for AI teams, what investors actually look for right now. 5. The Future of AI: Responsible Building & What Comes Next Ethics/responsible AI, agentic systems, what founders and engineers alike should be watching for in the next 1–2 years. Leaves the audience with a forward-looking note. Per-Topic Internal Split 40 min = 30 min discussion + 10 min Q&A. Repeats identically for all 5 topics. Segment Duration What Happens Moderator opens topic 2 min Brief framing of why this topic matters CG 1 turn ~5–6 min “[CG1 Name] sir/mam, what are your thoughts on [topic]?” CG 2 turn ~5–6 min “[CG2 Name] sir/mam, what's your take?” CG 3 turn ~5–6 min “[CG3 Name] sir/mam, what are your thoughts on this?” CG 4 turn ~5–6 min “[CG4 Name] sir/mam, what would you add?” CG 5 turn ~5–6 min “[CG5 Name] sir/mam, what are your thoughts on this?” Moderator synthesis ~2 min Quick wrap-up connecting the 5 viewpoints Audience Q&A 10 min Moderator picks 2–3 questions, directs to relevant chief guest Total: 30 min discussion + 10 min Q&A = 40 min
